en-us.html:
Protecting the people of Asgartha is the greatest honor.

Story

I take stock of the ranks of new recruits. They all know what's at stake. They are no longer cadets; they can now be awarded the rank of trooper, as long as they pass the two-year probation period. I know that most of them will join the Aegis contingents with flying colors. And even if some of them are rejected, they can still aspire to a logistics or administrative assistant post instead of returning to civilian life, and thus still earn their Rhombus that way. But there's another reason for the tense atmosphere. Many of them are hoping for a position within the Expeditionary Corps. Competition for these positions is particularly fierce, and only the best will succeed. I scan the ranks. The other instructors have placed bets on who will be the lucky ones that finish top of the class this year.

Even though I never stoop so low as to join in with such childish games, I can't help assessing my recruits. My role is also to decide who has what it takes to become an officer. Amensisa has that required firm hand, and Sunisa has that natural ease that makes her succeed in whatever she puts her mind to. But it's Osoyo that I feel has the most room for growth. The young man is not the most eloquent or the most talented, but he has patience and discipline. His is certainly a quiet strength, but he also has the hardness of steel in his eyes. He's always got his eyes on the objective, and he has a drive I've rarely seen in someone so young. The recruits stand to attention as the staff officers enter the mess. There's Admiral Singh, followed by Commodore Jeanty, and Sigismar as well. With a know-it-all smile on his face and a smugness that only nobility can produce, he scrutinizes each recruit in turn.

blocks.text: Protecting the people of Asgartha is the greatest honor.

blocks.title: Story

Competition for these positions is particularly fierce, and only the best will succeed. I scan the ranks. The other instructors have placed bets on who will be the lucky ones that finish top of the class this year.

Even though I never stoop so low as to join in with such childish games, I can't help assessing my recruits. My role is also to decide who has what it takes to become an officer. Amensisa has that required firm hand, and Sunisa has that natural ease that makes her succeed in whatever she puts her mind to. But it's Osoyo that I feel has the most room for growth. fr-fr.html:
Protéger le peuple d'Asgartha est le plus grand honneur qui soit.

Histoire

Je balaie du regard les rangs des nouvelles recrues. Toutes connaissent l'enjeu. Jadis cadets, elles peuvent désormais prétendre au rang de troupier, si elles passent les deux années probatoires. Je sais que la majorité d'entre elles réussiront à intégrer les contingents de l'Aegis haut la main. Et même si certaines sont recalées, ces dernières auront la possibilité, au lieu de retourner à la vie civile, de prétendre à un poste d'auxiliaire logistique ou administratif, et ainsi de gagner leur Losange... Mais si la tension est grande, c'est pour une autre raison. Elles sont nombreuses à briguer un poste au sein des Corps expéditionnaires. Et pour cette position particulière, les places seront chères, et seules les meilleures pourront y accéder. Je scanne l'assistance. Les autres instructeurs ont lancé les paris sur lesquelles auront cette chance, et qui sera major de promotion cette année.

Même si je ne m'abaisse jamais à participer à ce genre d'enfantillages, je ne peux m'empêcher de jauger mes recrues. Mon rôle sera aussi de déterminer qui parmi elles aura l'étoffe de passer officier. Amensisa a la poigne nécessaire, et Sunisa cette aisance naturelle qui lui fait tout réussir, mais c'est Osoyo qui a pour moi la meilleure marge de progression. Le jeune homme n'est peut-être pas le plus éloquent ni le plus talentueux, mais il a de la patience et de la discipline. C'est une force tranquille, certainement, mais il y a dans son regard la dureté de l'acier. Ses yeux sont en permanence fixés sur l'objectif, avec une niaque que j'ai rarement vue chez quelqu'un d'aussi jeune. Les recrues se mettent au garde-à-vous alors que les officiers d'état-major entrent dans le mess. L'Amiral Singh, suivie du Commodore Jeanty... mais aussi Sigismar. Un sourire pédant sur le visage, avec la suffisance que seule la noblesse peut allouer, il dévisage à son tour chaque recrue.
